In case of long waiting lists, the directorate of studies (SPL) will probably offer additional parallel courses, for which you may register. In this case, you will be able to change the number of points you allocated before a second allocation may start. If you gained an alternative place (remaining place) in a parallel course for which remaining places are available, you have to confirm or reject your place within the deadline defined by the directorate of studies (SPL). If you reject the place, you will be automatically deregistered from the course. The allocated points can not be transferred to the next semester. If you neither confirm nor reject the place, you remain on the waiting list. Related Links: - [Registration

retrieved.text:
Seminars, exercises e.g. are courses with continous assessment (German: prüfungsimmanente Lehrveranstaltung, pi LV). Courses with continuous assessment require a registration. In courses with continuous assessment attendance and other requirements are compulsory. The registration deadlines run in the weeks before the semester starts, i.e. in September for the winter semester and in February for the summer semester. If you are registered for a course with continuous assessment and are absent from the first unit without having a good reason, you will be deregistered.

With the registration to a course, you are automatically registered for the respective Moodle course.

- Some courses have a limited number of participants.
- Places are allocated in different ways, which are determined by each [directorate of studies](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273314) (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL)
- Either through the [point allocation system](#c600152), the [preference system](#c600156) or the [recommended path of study](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/recommended-path-of-study/).
- Students on the [waiting list](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273323) will be accepted if course places become available. In case you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course, as there may be free places.

retrieved.text: after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

- I want to take a module examination from an extension curriculum (EC) to fulfil the QZB. How should I proceed?

  In the non-degree programme you are only admitted to "attending individual courses". Therefore, you cannot register for the extension curriculum as such. You can only complete the examinations from an EC as lecture examination, not as module examinations. If you have any questions regarding the registration for examinations, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme.
- I am not able to register for a course. How shall I proceed?

  Please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the course is offered.
- How can I switch to the degree programme?

  You have successfully completed all courses and fulfil the required qualitative admission requirements in full? Apply for admission to the desired master programme in u:space within the application period. Your application will be processed according to the prevailing legal status at that time.

retrieved.title: Resumption of the same degree programme
heading_path: Bachelor programme without entrance exam procedure
retrieved.text: You were admitted to a bachelor programme without entrance exam procedure in the past, discontinued your studies and want to resume this degree programme? Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) within the application period and attach a scan of your passport or personal ID card. We will implement the resumption of studies within the admission period. Afterwards you will receive information about your further steps via e-mail. The processing may take several days. You do not have to submit an application for admission in u:space. You do not have to complete any Online-Self-Assessment and you do not need an online appointment. / - Bachelor/diploma programmes without entrance exam procedure - African Studies - Ancient History and Studies in Classical Antiquity - Astronomy - Byzantine Studies and Modern Greek Studies - Catholic Theology - Classical Archaeology - Classical Philology - Comparative Literature - Earth Sciences - Egyptology - European Ethnology - Geography - German Philology - History - History of Art - Hungarian and Finnish Studies - Islamic Theology - Japanology - Jewish Studies - Languages and Cultures of South Asia and Tibet - Linguistics - Mathematics - Meteorology and Climate Science - Musicology - Oriental Studies - Philosophy - Physics - Prehistory and Historical Archaeology - Protestant Theology - Religious Education - Romance Studies - Scandinavian Studies - Sinology - Slavonic Studies - Statistics and Data Analytics - Theatre, Film

reranked.title: Registration for courses with continuous assessment
reranked.text:
Seminars, exercises e.g. are courses with continous assessment (German: prüfungsimmanente Lehrveranstaltung, pi LV). Courses with continuous assessment require a registration. In courses with continuous assessment attendance and other requirements are compulsory. The registration deadlines run in the weeks before the semester starts, i.e. in September for the winter semester and in February for the summer semester. If you are registered for a course with continuous assessment and are absent from the first unit without having a good reason, you will be deregistered.

With the registration to a course, you are automatically registered for the respective Moodle course.

- Some courses have a limited number of participants.
- Places are allocated in different ways, which are determined by each [directorate of studies](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273314) (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL)
- Either through the [point allocation system](#c600152), the [preference system](#c600156) or the [recommended path of study](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/recommended-path-of-study/).
- Students on the [waiting list](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273323) will be accepted if course places become available. In case you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course, as there may be free places.

reranked.text: after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

reranked.title: Non-degree programme to make up for qualitative admission requirements
reranked.text:
/

- I want to take a module examination from an extension curriculum (EC) to fulfil the QZB. How should I proceed?

  In the non-degree programme you are only admitted to "attending individual courses". Therefore, you cannot register for the extension curriculum as such. You can only complete the examinations from an EC as lecture examination, not as module examinations. If you have any questions regarding the registration for examinations, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme.
- I am not able to register for a course. How shall I proceed?

  Please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the course is offered.
- How can I switch to the degree programme?

  You have successfully completed all courses and fulfil the required qualitative admission requirements in full? Apply for admission to the desired master programme in u:space within the application period. Your application will be processed according to the prevailing legal status at that time.

You can re-sit a passed exam once within twelve months (provided that you have not yet completed your degree programme/stage of the degree programme/module). With your new attempt, the previous examination result becomes void and the new grade is entered, even if the second grade is lower than the first. If no courses with continuous assessment are offered in the semester following your first attempt, you can only re-sit the exam in the semester after next at the latest, but not after you have completed the corresponding module, stage of the degree programme or degree programme. You should announce that you would like to re-sit the exam within 6 months. If you do not use this opportunity, you will not be able to repeat the exam at a later point. heading_path: Bachelor programme without entrance exam procedure
reranked.text: You were admitted to a bachelor programme without entrance exam procedure in the past, discontinued your studies and want to resume this degree programme? Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) within the application period and attach a scan of your passport or personal ID card. We will implement the resumption of studies within the admission period. Afterwards you will receive information about your further steps via e-mail. The processing may take several days. You do not have to submit an application for admission in u:space. selected.text: / - Waiting list and allocation of remaining places (courses) After the end of a registration deadline, you can check whether or not you gained a place on a lecture/an exam in u:space under Studies > Registration Overview. Furthermore, you receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. If you did not gain a place, you may be listed on the waiting list or be offered an alternative parallel course. If students deregister from a course/exam and a waiting list exists, you can move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. In case of long waiting lists, the directorate of studies (SPL) will probably offer additional parallel courses, for which you may register. In this case, you will be able to change the number of points you allocated before a second allocation may start. If you gained an alternative place (remaining place) in a parallel course for which remaining places are available, you have to confirm or reject your place within the deadline defined by the directorate of studies (SPL). If you reject the place, you will be automatically deregistered from the course. The allocated points can not be transferred to the next semester. If you neither confirm nor reject the place, you remain on the waiting list. Related Links: - [Registration
